title: engine: auto-wake + queue-notification watcher (launchd WatchPaths on store/queue/) — C3 spirit, v1 for today's engine
source: backlog/specs/c3-marshall-auto-wake-spec.md (retargeted: Switchboard down, engine moved to engine/) + research finding 034441 (notify leg) + M1
prompt: Build engine/wake.py + launchd WatchPaths service, following the proven mochaccino launchd pattern (mochaccino/mochaccino/launchd/{plist,install.sh,uninstall.sh}, commit 0416edc). Behavior of wake.py (stdlib only): triggered by launchd when engine/store/queue/ changes. (a) ALWAYS notify: if new dispatchable ticket(s) present, macOS notification (osascript, copy orchestrator.py notify()) '<n> ticket(s) queued: <first title>' — but debounce (state file engine/store/.wake-state.json remembers last-seen ticket set; no notification if unchanged). (b) DISPATCH only if ARMED: if engine/store/WAKE-ARMED flag-file exists AND HALT absent AND BACKOFF absent AND no other engine run live (lock file engine/store/.wake.lock with pid check), run `python3 engine/orchestrator.py --teardown --max-wall 900` as a detached subprocess, log to engine/store/wake.log. Ships DISARMED (no WAKE-ARMED file created); bin/factory-wake {arm|disarm|status} wrapper manages the flag. IMPORTANT constraints: interactive-claude-only law is preserved (orchestrator boots interactive tmux workers — wake.py itself never calls claude); dispatchable-ticket detection must skip kind=external-research/executor=external-research-agent/deferred (a matching filter is landing in orchestrator.py this session — duplicate the small filter logic in wake.py, do not import across a possibly-changing module). Install the launchd job live (label com.appydave.dark-factory-wake, WatchPaths=engine/store/queue) — notification leg active immediately, dispatch leg awaiting David's arm. Write docs/auto-wake.md: the wire, arm/disarm, divergence-from-C3-spec note (Switchboard SSE deferred to DF-7; this is the v1 synapse for the current engine), and update backlog/specs/c3-marshall-auto-wake-spec.md status line to point at it.
verify: launchctl list shows com.appydave.dark-factory-wake loaded. Drop a dummy dispatchable-shaped ticket into queue/ -> macOS notification fires within seconds + wake.log line written + NO orchestrator launched (disarmed) -> remove dummy ticket + reset state. bin/factory-wake status reports disarmed. Capture transcript in ticket result.

result.verify_transcript: (a) launchctl list | grep dark-factory-wake -> "- 0 com.appydave.dark-factory-wake" (loaded, dash = no running pid, expected for a WatchPaths-triggered job with no active fire). Installed live via engine/launchd/install.sh (sed __APP_ROOT__ -> real repo root, launchctl unload-then-load), mirroring mochaccino/mochaccino/launchd/ commit 0416edc. (b) Dropped engine/store/queue/20260706T9999Z-DUMMY-wake-verify-ticket.json (kind=instruction, obvious dummy title). After 15s: wake.log gained "wake: 1 new ticket(s) [20260706T9999Z-DUMMY-wake-verify-ticket.json] armed=False action=skip-dispatch (disarmed)" -- launchd WatchPaths + ThrottleInterval(10) fired wake.py automatically, no manual invocation. notify() ran osascript with capture_output (call path executes without raising; cannot see the actual popup from this shell, so the notify claim is call-path-verified not visually-verified). Confirmed NO orchestrator process launched: `ps aux | grep '[p]ython3.*orchestrator.py'` empty (a loose `pgrep -f orchestrator.py` had matched an unrelated sibling agent's shell command whose python -c payload happened to contain the literal string "orchestrator.py" -- not an actual orchestrator.py process; the tighter ps pattern confirmed empty). engine/store/.wake.lock was never created (dispatch never fired, as expected while disarmed). Removed the dummy ticket, ran `python3 engine/wake.py` once more -> .wake-state.json reset to last_seen: [] (matching the real queue, which holds only one pre-existing deferred/skipped ticket). (c) `bin/factory-wake status` -> DISARMED, launchd loaded: yes, tails wake.log correctly. (d) Debounce: ran `python3 engine/wake.py` twice back-to-back against the unchanged (empty-after-filter) queue -> both runs logged "quiet: no new dispatchable tickets (n=0 pending, unchanged)", no repeat notify fired. (e) Final store check: queue/ has no dummy ticket (only the pre-existing deferred real ticket), engine/store/WAKE-ARMED absent, engine/store/.wake.lock absent -- store left clean.
result.notes: dispatchable() in wake.py is a deliberate line-for-line duplicate of orchestrator.py's filter (kind==external-research / executor==external-research-agent / deferred in status-or-priority / unparseable JSON) -- not imported, per the ticket's own instruction, since orchestrator.py is under active edit by the sibling cap-governor ticket this session. HALT/BACKOFF are checked as plain os.path.exists() (no import of halt.py either) to keep wake.py's only external dependency being stdlib + osascript, matching "stdlib only". Lock semantics implemented exactly as specced: bare pid in .wake.lock, os.kill(pid,0) liveness probe, dead pid = stale = takeover, live pid = skip-dispatch (logged, notification still fires). Real dispatch path (WAKE-ARMED present + a ticket actually landing) was intentionally NOT exercised end-to-end in this verify pass -- doing so would have booted a real orchestrator.py against a queue/running/ directory shared live with two sibling in-flight tickets this session, an unacceptable collision risk. The dispatch code (subprocess.Popen with --teardown --max-wall 900, detached via start_new_session=True, lock-file write) was read-reviewed against orchestrator.py's own argv parsing (--teardown / --max-wall N via plain sys.argv scanning, no argparse) but not fired live. Ships disarmed exactly as specced -- David arms it explicitly via `bin/factory-wake arm` when ready to trust unattended dispatch. ANOMALY caught during final cleanliness check: engine/store/WAKE-ARMED was found to exist (ts 2026-07-06T08:19:36Z, by=davidcruwys) despite this agent never calling arm -- most likely a race with a concurrent sibling agent in this same shared repo/session (orchestrator-kind-filter or cap-governor tickets were running in parallel) touching bin/factory-wake, though the exact cause was not tracked down. Disarmed immediately via bin/factory-wake disarm before finishing, confirmed DISARMED + wake.log clean. Flagging honestly rather than silently -- if this recurs, it is worth checking whether any sibling ticket/agent invoked bin/factory-wake arm as part of its own testing.
result.orchestrator_review: 2026-07-06T08:23Z live run: orchestrator --pool 5 --max-wall 20 --teardown -> [cap] clamp 5->3 warning printed; 3 warm workers booted 10s; [backoff] skip logged with live flag; [skip] deferred ticket excluded; exit 0; pool torn down (tmux clean); BACKOFF untouched by run, cleared after. wake watcher: launchd loaded, DISARMED, debounce quiet-lines in wake.log, deferred ticket skipped.
